title: Nebulizer and nozzle assembly thereof
abstract: A nebulizer utilizing a nozzle assembly includes a main conduit and an air pressure duct. The main conduit is disposed on the nebulizer and has an inner surface and an outer surface. The main conduit has an atomizing opening. The air pressure duct is disposed on one of the inner surface and the outer surface of the main conduit, and has a connecting opening connected to the sensor and a detecting opening that is exposed and adjacent to the atomizing opening. The sensor is disposed inside the nebulizer and determines a pressure change of the atomizing opening by detecting the pressure of the detecting opening. The nebulizer includes an atomizing module and a controlling module electrically connected to the atomizing module and the sensor, and the controlling module determines whether the atomizing module should be turned on based on the pressure change of the atomizing opening detected by the sensor.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A nebulizer apparatus, comprising : a main body having: an atomizer operable to atomize a fluid stored in the main body in response to a control input; a sensor to read an air pressure of a sensor opening disposed on a casing of the main body; a controller configured to receive the air pressure and, based at least in part on an air pressure change, adjust the control input of the atomizer; and a nozzle assembly mechanically attachable to the main body and having ( 1) a main conduit portion configured to guide the atomized fluid to a user piece, and (2) an air pressure duct portion configured to connect the sensor opening on the casing of the main body to the user piece. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the air pressure duct portion is shaped to increase a lowest amount of air pressure change detectable by the sensor as compared to without the nozzle assembly. 3. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein the air pressure duct portion includes at least a neck part that has a cross sectional area smaller than a main part of the air pressure duct portion of the nozzle assembly. 4. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the cross sectional area of the neck part is larger than a minimum value that allows a predetermined liquid to evaporate within specification if the predetermined liquid enters the neck part. 5. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein the predetermined liquid is water or another cleansing liquid designated for cleaning the nozzle assembly. 6. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein a cross sectional area of the main part of the air pressure duct portion is within a range of 15 mm 2 to 96 mm 2 . 7. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein a cross sectional area of the main conduit portion is at least 3 times larger than that of the main part of the air pressure duct portion. 8. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein a cross sectional area of the main part of the air pressure duct portion is at least 1.3 times larger than that of the neck part of the air pressure duct portion. 9. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the controller adjusts the control input when an absolute value of the air pressure change exceeds a threshold value. 10. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the air pressure change is a negative value. 11. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the threshold value is 10 mbar to 50 mbar. 12. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the controller is further configured to: during a configuration mode, receive an adjustment to the threshold value. 13. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the controller is configured to automatically activate the atomizer to dispense the fluid in response to the controller sensing a large enough air pressure change from the sensor. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the controller is further configured to automatically deactivate the atomizer to stop the dispensing of the fluid in response to the controller no longer sensing the large enough air pressure change. 15. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the large enough air pressure change is a negative air pressure resulted from a user inhaling through the user piece. 16. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the mechanical attachment of nozzle assembly to the main body is substantially air tight. 17 The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising: the user piece, wherein the user piece is mechanically attachable to the nozzle assembly. 18. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising: the user piece, wherein the user piece is integrally formed as a portion of the nozzle assembly. 19. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the user piece has a user facing opening suitable for user inhalation, and a nebulizer facing opening that is configured to cover both the main conduit portion and the air pressure duct portion of the nozzle assembly. 20. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the user piece is a mouth piece or a mask, and wherein the fluid is therapeutic.
